characters.description: A Madra-linked Challenger who awakens alone in a Tier 4 trial with dangerously high density already packed into his body. Separated from his allies and uncertain whether they survived the war against the Graymin, he enters the arena determined to survive without absorbing any more.
characters.name: Jiran

characters.description: An unidentified feral being on the arena mountain that can move with extreme speed and rebuild its furred humanoid body from bones and smoke. It appears sentient and is connected to the vast column of mana rising from the peak.
characters.name: Mountain anomaly

characters.description: Jiran's partner and an allied commander helping to hold a damaged defensive line during his disappearance. She remains certain that he is alive and listens when Olive admits her feelings for him.
characters.name: Mayalyn

characters.description: An Imperial diviner and political leader helping defend the alliance while Jiran is missing. She confides in Mayalyn that she has romantic feelings for Jiran, though he has not yet answered them.
characters.name: Olive

characters.description: Another Tier 4 Challenger from Madra. The arena scoreboard places him first, ahead of Jiran, making him the only other contestant shown with a positive result.
characters.name: Kane

characters.description: A powerful telepathic entity who sponsors Jiran and contacts him after his Tier 4 trial. She judges him unworthy, and her mental presence overwhelms his defenses.
characters.name: Jiran's sponsor

characters.description: A dormant Lost host assigned to guard a sealed teleportation temple. A Found parasite controls his body and, speaking through it, claims that hosts willingly unite with the Found upon reaching adulthood.
characters.name: Jed

characters.description: A crystal-bearing captive held by Jiran in the wasteland. He explains how the local body-grown crystals manipulate mana and describes Seekers as feared collectors of information whose secrets are dangerous to discuss.
characters.name: Doric

characters.description: A Found operative sent aboard an experimental vessel to investigate the disturbance caused by Jiran in the wasteland.
characters.name: Seeker captain

characters.description: One of the captive Found hosts at the desert temple. She accepts Jiran's memory-altering Mana Venom treatment as a condition of survival.
characters.name: Tori

characters.description: A Timberling scout who greets Jiran when he returns to the Valley of Melodies. She reports the allied retreat through his portals and helps establish that Olive was captured during the Church assault.
characters.name: Knife

characters.description: An Imperial high Ranker and Jiran's mentor. During the battle with the Graymin, he exhausts his reserves and gravely injures himself using his Aspect of Balance to slow a devastating energy wave long enough to save the surrounding Rankers.
characters.name: Lenton

characters.description: The public identity Jiran used among Imperial forces. When he takes command of the soldiers in the valley, he openly confirms that Mortimer is an alias and gives them his real name.
characters.name: Mortimer

characters.description: Jiran’s cousin, a healer and combat-support specialist serving the allied force. Timberling leaders credit her with reviving wounded fighters.
characters.name: Niya

characters.description: Jiran's friend and an ally concerned for Olive. He believes the Church is likely to keep her alive because of her political value, and he joins the group preparing to recover her.
characters.name: Cameron

aliases: Leader of the hostile Church faction
characters.description: The senior Church leader holding Olive in Church custody. He seeks to control both her confinement and her public position, using political pressure and sealed formations while presenting her captivity through formal Church authority.
characters.name: The cardinal

characters.description: An imperial prince aligned with the Church. At a gathering arranged around Olive's captivity, he credits the Church for the recent military victory and dismisses the contribution of Olive's soldiers and allies.
characters.name: Deacon Matthias

characters.description: Commander of the combined Imperial Defense Forces and the Fifth Imperial Corps for the Sanctum operation. He receives nearly two hundred thousand soldiers with orders to provide a nonaggressive distraction and withdraw if the rescue party fails.
characters.name: Colonel Ropeter

characters.description: An Imperial officer involved in preparing the force deployed near Sanctum. He initially objects to Jiran's condition that the new weapons cannot harm allied peoples, then accepts it.
characters.name: Colonel Mart

characters.description: A divinatory presence connected to Jiran and Madra. She confirms that Olive is somewhere in Sanctum, but cannot determine the precise location because a barrier blocks closer examination.
characters.name: Daughter

characters.description: A former bandit ruler and late Tier VI ascender whom Jiran encounters inside Sanctum. He says he has come to expose a major Church operation and claims that Silence secretly performs work the Church cannot conduct openly.
characters.name: Markhiss

characters.description: A powerful Ranker and Jiran's returning mentor. He destroys the green spectral threat attacking Olive and discovers that five of her siblings are imprisoned nearby with white bones implanted in their bodies.
characters.name: Feylon Samris

characters.description: A veteran female Ranker who mistakes Jiran's pure mana ice body for one of the beasts attacking Sanctum and shatters it with a strike.
characters.name: Veteran female Ranker

characters.description: Olive's younger sister, found alive but unresponsive after Church captivity. Her condition leaves the extent of her injuries and recovery uncertain.
characters.name: Melania

characters.description: The world-linked source of mana known to Jiran from earlier events. Her nature and agency remain uncertain, but Jiran understands his soul connection and obligations in relation to her before this volume begins.
characters.name: Madra

characters.description: A famous Imperial healer whom Niya admired as a child. The cardinal mortally wounds her with an emperors’ dagger during the fighting in Sanctum, and Jiran cannot save her.
characters.name: Renowned Imperial healer

characters.description: A high-tier Imperial survivor and Olive’s brother. Though already injured, he enters the Tier X beast’s lightning to recover Jiran’s burned body.
characters.name: Olive’s brother

characters.description: A semi-sapient Tier X entity directing the undead assault on Sanctum. It can possess victims, corrupt mana, and manipulate its forces from a distance.
characters.name: Tier X corruption beast

characters.description: One of three high Rankers caught in the emperors' rupture of reality. She awakens alone in an unfamiliar jungle with her aura and physical strength severely suppressed, facing dangerous local creatures.
characters.name: Isolated high Ranker

characters.description: An Imperial commander who confronts Jiran after soldiers under his authority steal weapons reserved for allied peoples. Jiran strips away his mana and removes him from command.
characters.name: Hostile Imperial general

characters.description: The two rulers traveling with three high Rankers after concluding that their ascent to Tier 11 endangers the world's weave. They entrust the empire's people to their companions, rupture reality together, and are scattered when the fissure engulfs the group.
characters.name: Two emperors
